flashcards.back: The minuend is the initial quantity or number in a subtraction equation from which another number is subtracted. In $a - b = c$, $a$ is the minuend.
The subtrahend is the quantity or number that is being subtracted or taken away. In $a - b = c$, $b$ is the subtrahend.
The difference is the result of subtracting one number from another. In $a - b = c$, $c$ is the difference.
No. Subtraction is not commutative because changing the order of the numbers changes the result ($a - b \neq b - a$).
It is the process of taking 1 unit from a higher place value column to the left (e.g., taking 1 ten and turning it into 10 ones) to subtract a larger digit from a smaller one.
